capabilities.name: Registration
capabilities.verify: register_webinar skill (rpc:register_for_webinar, scope external); registrations view in WebinarsPage

capabilities.name: Reminder emails
capabilities.verify: LIVE E2E VERIFIED on rzhj 2026-07-05 (Stage-3): created webinar + registration via skills, send_webinar_reminders returned confirm.sent=1 (confirmation email delivered through email-send) and an IMMEDIATE second run returned confirm.sent=0 — proving reminder_confirm_sent_at was stamped and dedupes (marker unreadable directly due to the manage_webinar registrations bug, so the double-run IS the marker proof). Windows mirror webinar_reminder_tick (confirm/T-24h/T-1h/post); 'Webinar Reminders' cron automation every 15 min (migration 20260705160000). DELIVERY BUILT 2026-07-05 (was 'in schema, not wired': webinar_reminder_tick existed but nothing called it and nothing turned its events into emails). New send-webinar-reminders edge function sweeps the same four windows (confirm / T-24h / T-1h / post), emails via email-send, and stamps the per-registration markers; 'Webinar Reminders' cron automation (every 15 min, agent_automations via migration 20260705160000) drives it through automation-dispatcher; send_webinar_reminders skill registered (module seed + migration). Tick remains as the event-emitting alternative — both dedupe on the same markers. Stays partial pending deploy + a Stage-3 live sweep run.
capabilities.notes: Delivery built + live-verified same day. T-24h/T-1h legs share the identical query/send/stamp path (windows too far out to exercise in-test); the confirm and post legs were both fired live.

capabilities.name: Attendance tracking
capabilities.verify: mark_webinar_attendance skill; attendance handling in WebinarsPage

capabilities.name: Recording links
capabilities.verify: complete_webinar skill accepts p_recording_url; recording shown in WebinarsPage

capabilities.name: Follow-up emails
capabilities.verify: LIVE E2E VERIFIED on rzhj 2026-07-05 (Stage-3): past-dated webinar marked completed via complete_webinar with an unattended registration → send_webinar_reminders returned post.sent=1 (missed_you variant follow-up email) and a second run returned post.sent=0 (marker + follow_up_sent stamped in the same update).
capabilities.notes: The post email IS the follow-up: thanks vs missed_you by attended, recording link when set, stamps webinar_registrations.follow_up_sent=true (the WebinarsPage badge flag).

capabilities.name: Paid tickets
capabilities.verify: no price/ticket fields in webinars schema or WebinarsPage
